resposta:
solution: 
```python
class Solution:
    def canBeEqual(self, target: List[int], arr: List[int]) -> bool:
        return sorted(target) == sorted(arr)
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n log n)
space complexity: O(1) extra (sorting in-place)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: Any permutation of the array can be reached via a sequence of subarray reversals, so we only need to check if the multisets of elements match. Sorting both arrays and comparing them is the simplest and fastest way to do this.
